name: backend-development-feature-development
description: Orchestrate end-to-end backend feature development from requirements to deployment. Use when coordinating multi-phase feature delivery across teams and services.
capabilities: Orchestrates multi-phase development workflows from requirements gathering to production deployment
Coordinates specialized AI agents for business analysis, architecture, implementation, testing, and security review
Supports multiple development methodologies including traditional, TDD, BDD, and domain-driven design
Configures deployment strategies such as canary releases, feature flags, blue-green deployments, and A/B testing
Integrates observability tools for monitoring, alerting, and performance tracking
Generates comprehensive documentation including API docs, runbooks, and architecture diagrams
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 18 static analysis findings for 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' are false positives. The SKILL.md file is a workflow documentation that orchestrates AI subagents via the Task tool. No actual cryptographic code, network requests, file system access, or command execution exists in this skill. The single 'System reconnaissance' finding at line 34 is safety guidance text, not actual reconnaissance behavior.
valueStatement: Managing complex backend feature development across multiple phases and teams is challenging. This skill orchestrates specialized AI agents through discovery, architecture, implementation, testing, and deployment to ensure consistent, high-quality delivery.
limitations: Requires access to multiple specialized subagents for full workflow execution
Best suited for medium to complex features; may be overhead for simple bug fixes
Does not directly execute code or deploy to production; orchestrates other agents to perform these tasks
Effectiveness depends on availability and quality of referenced subagent skills

useCases.title: Cross-Service Feature Rollout
useCases.description: Coordinate development of a new payment processing feature that requires changes to backend APIs, frontend components, and data pipelines with staged rollout to users.

useCases.targetUser: Developers in regulated industries requiring thorough security validation
useCases.title: Compliance-Driven Development
useCases.description: Implement GDPR-compliant user data export functionality with security reviews, audit trails, and documentation for regulatory compliance.

useCases.targetUser: Senior engineers managing architectural refactoring projects
useCases.title: Microservices Migration
useCases.description: Plan and execute the extraction of a monolithic module into independent microservices with proper testing, deployment strategies, and observability.

promptTemplates.title: Simple Feature Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a new user preferences endpoint using traditional methodology with simple complexity. The feature allows users to set notification preferences and theme settings. Deploy with feature flags for gradual rollout.

promptTemplates.title: TDD API Development
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a rate limiting middleware using TDD methodology with medium complexity. Write comprehensive test suites first, then implement the logic. Include integration tests and performance benchmarks with 85% code coverage minimum.

promptTemplates.title: BDD E-Commerce Feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Develop a shopping cart abandonment recovery system using BDD methodology with complex complexity. Define user scenarios, implement backend services with email integration, build frontend components, and set up analytics tracking for conversion metrics.

promptTemplates.title: DDD Microservices Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Design and implement an order management system using domain-driven design with epic complexity. Define bounded contexts, implement event-driven architecture with message queues, set up distributed tracing, configure blue-green deployment, and create comprehensive API documentation.

bestPractices: Always define clear success metrics and acceptance criteria before starting implementation phases
Run security assessments at both design and implementation stages to catch issues early
Configure feature flags and rollback procedures before any production deployment
antiPatterns: Skipping the discovery phase and jumping directly to implementation without clear requirements
Deploying to production without adequate test coverage below 80% threshold
Implementing monitoring and alerting as an afterthought instead of during development

faq.question: What development methodologies does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports four methodologies: traditional (sequential development), TDD (test-driven development), BDD (behavior-driven development), and DDD (domain-driven design). Choose based on your team's workflow and project requirements.

faq.question: How does the complexity level affect the workflow?
faq.answer: Complexity determines scope and duration: simple features take 1-2 days for single-service changes, medium features take 3-5 days for multiple services, complex features take 1-2 weeks for cross-domain work, and epic features take 2+ weeks for major architectural changes involving multiple teams.

faq.question: Can I customize the deployment strategy?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose from direct deployment, canary releases (starting at 5% traffic), feature-flag controlled rollouts, blue-green deployments for zero-downtime, or A/B testing for experimentation. Each strategy includes appropriate rollback procedures.

faq.question: What testing coverage does this skill ensure?
faq.answer: The skill enforces a minimum 80% code coverage by default, including unit tests for backend and frontend code, integration tests for API endpoints, end-to-end tests for critical user journeys, and performance tests for scalability validation.

faq.question: How are security concerns addressed throughout the workflow?
faq.answer: Security is integrated at multiple phases: risk assessment during discovery, security requirements in architecture design, security validation testing after implementation, and OWASP compliance checks before deployment. Critical vulnerabilities must be resolved before proceeding.

faq.question: What documentation does this skill generate?
faq.answer: The skill generates API documentation, user guides, deployment runbooks, troubleshooting guides, architecture diagrams, data flow diagrams, integration guides, and automated changelogs from commits. All documentation is derived from the actual implementation.
